Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. A Visual Introduction to Machine Learning (2015) 184 upvotes, 10 comments
  2. Human Organ Atlas 43 upvotes, 3 comments
  3. Examples for the tcpdump and dig man pages 56 upvotes, 6 comments
  4. SBCL Fibers – Lightweight Cooperative Threads 130 upvotes, 24 comments
  5. Glassworm Is Back: A New Wave of Invisible Unicode Attacks Hits Repositories 32 upvotes, 3 comments
  6. Rack-mount hydroponics 260 upvotes, 57 comments
  7. Bumblebee queens breathe underwater to survive drowning 171 upvotes, 39 comments
  8. Generating All 32-Bit Primes (Part I) 44 upvotes, 8 comments
  9. Kniterate Notes 14 upvotes, 1 comments
  10. IBM, sonic delay lines, and the history of the 80×24 display 42 upvotes, 9 comments
  11. Show HN: Signet – Autonomous wildfire tracking from satellite and weather data 54 upvotes, 13 comments
  12. Pentagon expands oversight of Stars and Stripes, limits content 96 upvotes, 29 comments
  13. Why Mathematica does not simplify sinh(arccosh(x)) 108 upvotes, 36 comments
  14. A most elegant TCP hole punching algorithm 157 upvotes, 57 comments
  15. Allow me to get to know you, mistakes and all 229 upvotes, 99 comments
  16. Centuries of selective breeding turned wild cabbage into different vegetables 94 upvotes, 35 comments
  17. Show HN: What if your synthesizer was powered by APL (or a dumb K clone)? 25 upvotes, 7 comments
  18. Show HN: Han – A Korean programming language written in Rust 197 upvotes, 105 comments
  19. Slicing Bezier Surfaces 27 upvotes, 11 comments
  20. What makes Intel Optane stand out (2023) 21 upvotes, 8 comments
  21. Small U.S. town, big company. Can it weather the tariff Blizzard? (Digi-Key) (2025) 41 upvotes, 22 comments
  22. $96 3D-printed rocket that recalculates its mid-air trajectory using a $5 sensor 250 upvotes, 192 comments
  23. The Appalling Stupidity of Spotify's AI DJ 291 upvotes, 238 comments
  24. How kernel anti-cheats work 274 upvotes, 226 comments
  25. MCP is dead; long live MCP 219 upvotes, 179 comments
  26. A Theory of the World as run by large adult children 189 upvotes, 194 comments
  27. Trust no one: are one-way trusts one way? 3 upvotes, 0 comments
  28. 100 hour gap between a vibecoded prototype and a working product 120 upvotes, 131 comments
  29. The Official DR DOS Website 8 upvotes, 4 comments
  30. Treasure hunter freed from jail after refusing to turn over shipwreck gold 138 upvotes, 190 comments

Last generated 12:00am, 16 March 2026. Source here.
